title: ExportBundle
description: RFC 0098 §B. A portable agent-platform export bundle — a tenant's reusable estate (agents, packs, prompt templates, connection refs, schedules, roster/org-chart) composed for migration between openwop hosts (or import from an external platform via an adapter). The bundle MUST NOT contain credential VALUES: `connection-ref` items carry only refs/provider ids per RFC 0046/0079; the importer reports unbound refs in `secretsToRebind` and re-binds secrets at the destination. All imported entities are re-owned to the caller's RFC 0048 identity; `source.originPrincipal` is informational only and grants no access. Gated on the `portability` capability.

bundleVersion.description: Bundle schema version. Importers MUST reject a bundleVersion they do not understand.

source.description: Provenance of the bundle. Informational only — never a source of authority at the destination.

origin.description: Origin host base URL or adapter id (e.g. 'adapter:openclaw').

originPrincipal.description: Opaque source identity, informational only. MUST NOT grant any access at the destination.

items.description: The estate, in arbitrary order; `dependsOn` edges define apply order (topological; a cycle is a 422).

ref.description: Stable id within the bundle, used as the target of `dependsOn` edges.

dependsOn.description: Refs of other items that MUST be applied before this one (topological order).

payload.description: The kind's existing schema (RFC 0070 manifest, 0003/0013 pack, 0027 template, 0045/0095 connection *ref*, 0052 job, 0086 roster, 0087 org-chart). Deliberately open here because each kind validates against its own schema. For `connection-ref`, the payload MUST carry only refs/provider ids — a literal credential value is rejected (422).
payload.$comment: Intentionally open object — validated against the kind's own schema; connection-ref payloads MUST NOT contain credential values (export-bundle-no-credential-material).
